card.indications: Central diabetes insipidus, nocturnal enuresis or nocturia with selected formulations, and treatment of bleeding in mild hemophilia A or type 1 von Willebrand disease.
card.mechanism: Acts primarily at renal V2 receptors to increase water reabsorption and can increase release of factor VIII and von Willebrand factor.
card.dosing: Dose is indication-, route-, and formulation-specific. Use the lowest effective dose and reduce or avoid use in renal impairment.
card.routeAdministration: Verify formulation carefully because products are not interchangeable. Restrict fluids as directed and monitor sodium closely.
card.contraindications: Hyponatremia or history of hyponatremia, moderate to severe renal impairment for many products, and hypersensitivity.
card.warnings: Can cause severe hyponatremia, water intoxication, seizures, coma, and death. Risk increases with excess fluid intake, illness, older age, and interacting drugs.
card.commonAdverseEffects: Headache, nausea, flushing, nasal irritation, and mild fluid retention.
card.seriousAdverseEffects: Severe hyponatremia, seizures, cerebral edema, thrombosis, and anaphylaxis.
card.interactions: SSRIs, thiazides, carbamazepine, NSAIDs, and other drugs that promote hyponatremia increase risk.
card.monitoring: Monitor serum sodium, fluid intake, urine output, weight, renal function, and clinical response.
card.patientTeaching: Follow fluid restrictions exactly and report headache, confusion, nausea, sudden weight gain, or seizures immediately.
card.nutrition: Limit fluids during the ordered restriction period and avoid excess alcohol because it alters water balance.

card.indications: Treatment of anemia due to chronic kidney disease, chemotherapy, zidovudine therapy, and reduction of transfusion needs in selected surgical patients.
card.mechanism: Stimulates erythroid progenitor cells in bone marrow to increase red-blood-cell production.
card.dosing: Dose is based on indication, hemoglobin response, weight, and transfusion risk. Use the lowest dose sufficient to reduce transfusions.
card.routeAdministration: Do not shake. Use correct product and concentration. Ensure adequate iron stores before and during therapy.
card.contraindications: Uncontrolled hypertension and pure red cell aplasia after erythropoietin therapy.
card.warnings: Boxed warnings include death, myocardial infarction, stroke, venous thromboembolism, tumor progression, and shortened survival when hemoglobin is raised too aggressively.
card.commonAdverseEffects: Hypertension, headache, arthralgia, fever, injection-site reaction, and nausea.
card.seriousAdverseEffects: Thrombosis, stroke, myocardial infarction, seizures, hypertensive crisis, and pure red cell aplasia.
card.interactions: No major metabolic interactions; treatment response depends on iron, folate, vitamin B12, inflammation, and blood loss.
card.monitoring: Monitor hemoglobin, blood pressure, iron studies, CBC, and signs of thrombosis or seizures.
card.patientTeaching: Keep blood and blood-pressure appointments and report chest pain, sudden weakness, severe headache, leg swelling, or shortness of breath.
card.nutrition: Maintain adequate iron, folate, vitamin B12, and protein intake as prescribed.

card.indications: Treatment and prevention of cytomegalovirus infection in high-risk or immunocompromised patients; ophthalmic products treat CMV retinitis or herpetic keratitis depending on formulation.
card.mechanism: Phosphorylated in infected cells and inhibits viral DNA polymerase, terminating viral DNA synthesis.
card.dosing: Weight- and renal-function based. Induction and maintenance regimens differ. Adjust promptly for renal impairment.
card.routeAdministration: Use hazardous-drug precautions. Infuse IV doses over the recommended time and avoid rapid bolus administration.
card.contraindications: Severe hypersensitivity to ganciclovir or valganciclovir.
card.warnings: Can cause severe neutropenia, anemia, thrombocytopenia, infertility, fetal harm, carcinogenicity, and renal injury.
card.commonAdverseEffects: Fever, diarrhea, nausea, headache, weakness, and injection-site inflammation.
card.seriousAdverseEffects: Bone-marrow suppression, severe infection, hemorrhage, seizures, renal failure, and anaphylaxis.
card.interactions: Other myelosuppressive or nephrotoxic drugs increase toxicity. Probenecid may increase ganciclovir exposure.
card.monitoring: Monitor CBC with differential, platelets, renal function, infection response, and neurologic symptoms.
card.patientTeaching: Report fever, unusual bruising, bleeding, severe fatigue, or reduced urine. Use effective contraception as directed.
card.nutrition: Maintain hydration unless restricted and support adequate calorie and protein intake during treatment.

card.indications: Prevention and treatment of nausea and vomiting related to chemotherapy, radiation, or surgery depending on formulation.
card.mechanism: Blocks serotonin 5-HT3 receptors in the vagal nerve terminals and chemoreceptor trigger zone.
card.dosing: Dose and timing depend on chemotherapy, radiation, surgery, and formulation. Use product-specific directions.
card.routeAdministration: Apply patches to clean, dry, intact skin well before chemotherapy as directed. Administer IV doses slowly according to labeling.
card.contraindications: Hypersensitivity to granisetron or other 5-HT3 antagonists.
card.warnings: QT prolongation, serotonin syndrome, severe constipation, and masking of progressive ileus may occur.
card.seriousAdverseEffects: Arrhythmia, serotonin syndrome, bowel obstruction, and anaphylaxis.
card.interactions: Other serotonergic or QT-prolonging drugs increase risk.
card.monitoring: Monitor nausea control, bowel function, ECG in at-risk patients, electrolytes, and signs of serotonin syndrome.
card.patientTeaching: Report palpitations, fainting, severe constipation, agitation, sweating, or muscle rigidity.
card.nutrition: Use small frequent meals and adequate fluids as tolerated; avoid foods that worsen nausea.

card.indications: Treatment of selected malignancies and chronic viral infections depending on current product labeling and combination regimen.
card.mechanism: Binds cell-surface interferon receptors and alters gene transcription, producing antiviral, antiproliferative, and immunomodulating effects.
card.dosing: Highly indication-specific and often weight- or body-surface-area based. Dose reductions may be required for cytopenias, hepatic dysfunction, or severe adverse effects.
card.routeAdministration: Use exact product, route, and regimen. Rotate subcutaneous sites and follow handling or refrigeration directions.
card.contraindications: Autoimmune hepatitis and severe decompensated liver disease in some regimens; hypersensitivity.
card.warnings: Can cause severe depression or suicidality, autoimmune disease, hepatic failure, bone-marrow suppression, infection, cardiac toxicity, and neurologic effects.
card.commonAdverseEffects: Flu-like symptoms, fatigue, fever, chills, myalgia, anorexia, and injection-site reactions.
card.seriousAdverseEffects: Suicidal behavior, severe cytopenias, liver failure, arrhythmia, seizures, autoimmune disorders, and severe infection.
card.interactions: Other myelosuppressive, hepatotoxic, or neuropsychiatric drugs may increase toxicity.
card.monitoring: Monitor CBC, liver tests, thyroid function, mental health, infection, weight, and cardiac status when indicated.
card.patientTeaching: Expect flu-like symptoms. Report depression, suicidal thoughts, chest pain, jaundice, unusual bleeding, or high fever promptly.
card.nutrition: Maintain hydration, protein, and calorie intake; manage nausea and appetite loss with small frequent meals.
